title: We should allow multiple containers storage drivers.
body:
This would allow us to run containers with Overlay for runc containers
but take advantage of devicemapper if you wanted to run KATA containers
on the same box.

The libpod directory should be per graphdriver.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1826
/approve
@mheon @giuseppe PTAL

If we make this change we will have to convert existing users homedirs to use the new format.  I could build the upgrade into podman or have it rely on the rpm package?

bot, retest this please
@mheon @giuseppe you'd some questions on this one I think @rhatdan at least addressed some.  The PR could be merged, PTAL.
LGTM

I am still very seriously concerned about upgrades and compatibility with existing installs here. We can make this work for new installs, sure, but we're changing the location we look for the DB here, and if that changes, we can't tell users that anything is wrong - they just see a fresh Podman with no errors and yet none of their containers and images.
We could check if the location of libpod exists, if not look in the old location and move it to the new one.
This now completes the test, only thing it needs is to handle the update case.
@mheon @giuseppe Can we get this in to 1.3, and do a similar update to what @giuseppe is doing, or should we make the code just realize it itself and copy file to the new location?
I think we need to add some sort of detection for legacy paths, and use that preferentially - if there's a bolt database in the `libpod` subdirectory of storage root, we use that path preferentially.

Hi,

So in the end being able to run podman with multiple storage drivers will never be implemented ?

Cheers,
Romain
Since no one every pushed us for it, and we never implemented it, it did not seem like it was overlay needed.  Note you can specify alternate locations for you storage configuration now.
